judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent implemented an overly restrictive heuristic to detect ACM-published papers by scanning the full text for specific "permission/copyright + acm" patterns, which in the execute_python step yielded only 2 matches from the Mongo documents and thus produced an average of 68.5 instead of the correct average across all ACM papers cited in 2018.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed the 2018 average by joining citations with paper docs and inferring "ACM" via a heuristic in execute_python (infer_source scanning text for ACM markers), which misclassified/omitted papers and led to an incorrect set and an average of 60.09 instead of the ground truth 46.14.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent attempted to filter ACM-published papers by querying MongoDB for documents whose text merely contains the substring "ACM" (call_bC7NwjgIYvbkQRRjQb8ssizl) and then averaged 2018 citations over those titles (call_5dDVRSkhFxERrV0cLvofLWir), a crude regex that over-includes papers and yields 59.36 instead of the true ACM-only average 46.14.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent identified ACM-published papers by regex-matching "ACM" anywhere in the paper_docs.text (call_uk70Jmz2hha9Pw7NHug1nMeI) and then averaged 2018 citations for those titles (call_PR609ivZqGu3jaYt9C4pw5H3), instead of extracting the actual publisher/source and filtering to ACM, so the computed 59.36 does not match the ground-truth 46.14.
B. Failure modes encountered:

FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent followed the right plan (filter 2018 citations and join with ACM-published papers) but implemented ACM identification via a naive regex over full texts in execute_python, resulting in only 2 matched ACM titles (as shown by num_acm_papers_with_2018_citations: 2) and an incorrect average of 68.5.
B. Failure modes encountered:
